tldr: Migrate from Adyen's enterprise unified payments platform to 2Checkout for SaaS and e-commerce billing, with the heavy lifting concentrated in a PCI-compliant token transfer and an integration rewrite.
why_switch: Adyen's enterprise model can feel heavy for lower-volume SaaS and digital-goods workloads
2Checkout is positioned around digital goods, subscriptions, and global online checkout
Move away from negotiated interchange++ contracts toward a simpler merchant relationship
Consolidate global tax, VAT, and compliance handling under a merchant-of-record model
Reduce the operational overhead of maintaining an enterprise PSP integration
what_to_export: Stored payment methods and tokens (handled via PCI-compliant transfer, not raw export)
Historical transactions, refunds, and chargeback records
Webhook endpoint inventory and event types in use
Product or SKU catalog and current pricing
Payout and settlement reports for reconciliation
API keys and integration credential inventory
export_method: Use Adyen's Customer Area reporting and CSV export tools to pull recurring payment metadata, transaction history, refunds, disputes, and payout reports. For tokenized cards, do not attempt a self-serve download — request a processor-to-processor migration through Adyen support, since card networks generally require a PCI-compliant transfer between vaults. Capture webhook configurations, signing keys, and any custom risk rules so the new integration can be reproduced. Confirm scope, timing, and required documentation with Adyen's official migration team before scheduling cutover.
import_method: Onboard a 2Checkout merchant account, complete KYC, and import customers, products, and subscriptions through their merchant dashboard or API. Coordinate the PCI-compliant card token migration between Adyen and 2Checkout's vault provider — both vendors must be involved and the work is contractual rather than client-driven. Re-point checkout flows and webhook handlers to 2Checkout endpoints, re-implement signature verification, and run parallel processing for at least one full billing cycle to validate. Verify the exact API surface, supported payment methods, and import endpoints in the current 2Checkout official documentation before cutover.

concept_mapping.note: Map identifier and persist a mapping table during cutover
Requires PCI-compliant token migration, not direct copy
Different session lifecycle — re-implement client integration
Event names and payload shapes differ; re-test all consumers
Reconcile reporting fields manually against finance records
Confirm rates with sales — consult target's official pricing page and any written quote

gotchas: Card token migration requires a PCI-compliant processor-to-processor transfer; coordinate with both vendors early
2Checkout pricing is not transparent — consult target's official pricing page and obtain a written quote before committing
Confirm SDK and library currency directly with 2Checkout support and verify in current docs rather than relying on third-party signals
Merchant-of-record vs gateway models differ; tax remittance and refund flows may change semantically
Subscription proration, trials, and dunning logic rarely map 1:1 — re-test every plan and edge case
Run dual processing for at least one full billing cycle before decommissioning Adyen

email.subject: Migration plan: Adyen to 2Checkout
email.body:
Hi team,

I'd like to move our payments from Adyen to 2Checkout. Adyen's enterprise model is heavier than we need at our current TPV, and 2Checkout's SaaS/e-commerce focus matches our use case better.

The critical piece is the PCI-compliant token transfer between vaults — that has to be coordinated with both vendors, not done by us. We will export customers, subscriptions, and reports from Adyen, import to 2Checkout, run parallel for one full billing cycle, then cut over. Estimated 3-8 weeks.

Before we commit, I want a written quote from 2Checkout (their pricing isn't published) and confirmation from their support team that the SDKs we depend on are actively maintained against current docs.

Green light?
